evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Build data models following engineering best practices including testing, versioning, and CI/CD
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Responsibilities repeatedly emphasise testing, quality standards, and anomaly detection with no velocity/speed language, making this rigour-dominated.
evidence.domain_risk: Financial domain knowledge (P&L, FX, exposure, reconciliation)
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The role is explicitly for an international fintech company and requires P&L, FX, and reconciliation knowledge, an override signal for high domain risk.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Contribute to continuous improvement of development and quality standards
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named engineering practices (dbt, CI/CD, testing, Airflow/Cloud Composer) already exist in the responsibilities and the role improves/extends them, indicating a mid-stage function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Monitor data quality and propose alert systems and anomaly detection mechanisms
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Precise technical practices (anomaly detection, Cloud Composer, Domain-Driven Design, P&L/FX/reconciliation) give a concrete picture of daily work, consistent with hiring-manager authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Financial domain knowledge (P&L, FX, exposure, reconciliation)
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role is explicitly positioned for Data & Finance within a fintech company, with P&L/FX/reconciliation named as core domain knowledge, a finance orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: Lead end-to-end projects from definition through delivery
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role leads projects end-to-end (strategic) but also collaborates with stakeholders to convert scoped needs into solutions (execution), giving it a mixed autonomy profile.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools for the candidate to use or AI systems the candidate's output feeds.
evidence.testing_framing: Build data models following engineering best practices including testing, versioning, and CI/CD
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing is named as an owned engineering practice the candidate builds into their models, not merely a tool in a skill list.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Monitor data quality and propose alert systems and anomaly detection mechanisms
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: The role centers on catching data quality issues via anomaly detection before they affect financial reconciliation, a high-trust risk framing reinforced by the fintech/finance domain.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Design and develop reliable, scalable data products with business impact focus
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role both designs new data products and monitors/improves existing quality standards, a mix of greenfield and fix/scale work.
evidence.loss_aversion: Monitor data quality and propose alert systems and anomaly detection mechanisms — anomaly detection exists specifically to catch bad financial data before it causes reconciliation issues.
evidence.work_arrangement: Work Arrangement: Hybrid - 4 days office / 1 day remote
